LAW AND MOTION 302, OSC RE: PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION: NEITHER PARTY MAY PAY EACH OTHER OR THEMSELVES OR ANY OF THEIR BUSINESS AFFILIATES WITH MONEY FROM THE RESTAURANT. THE RESTAURANT SHALL BE OPERATED BY MR. KARAJAH. AND IF THE CASE IS DISMISSED, THE COURT'S ORDER EVAPORATES. [THE COURT'S COMPLETE RULING IS REFLECTED IN THE COURT REPORTER'S TRANSCRIPT.] COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT SHALL SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; REPORTER: REYES F. HUNTER, CSR#6576, RHUNTER@SFTC.ORG.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ISMAIL KARAJAH'S MOTION TO STAY DISSOLUTION AND FIX VALUE OF SHARES OF SHAREHOLDERS SEEKING DISSOLUTION IS OFF CALENDAR. THE PARTIES ELECTED TO MEET, CONFER AND RESOLVE ISSUES WITH THE ASSISTANCE OF THE COURT'S CIVIL CASE SETTLEMENT SPECIALIST, PANG LY. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: J. RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED. (L&M302)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT ISMAIL KARAJAH'S MOTION TO STAY DISSOLUTION AND FIX VALUE OF SHARES OF SHAREHOLDERS SEEKING DISSOLUTION IS CONTINUED TO SEPTEMBER 27, 2017 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPARTMENT 302. THE HEARING ON THIS MOTION IS CONTINUED TO GIVE PLAINTIFF NASER ODEH AN OPPORTUNITY TO FILE A MOTION PER CORPORATIONS CODE 2000(F) TO SET A DIFFERENT VALUATION DATE AND TO GIVE BOTH PARTIES THE OPPORTUNITY TO PROVIDE EVIDENCE REGARDING THE AMOUNT OF THE BOND THAT MR. KARAJAH NEEDS TO POST. MR. ODEH SHOULD NOTICE HIS VALUATION DATE MOTION TO BE HEARD ON SEPTEMBER 27, 2017. THAT MOTION SHOULD ALSO ADDRESS THE BOND AMOUNT. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR	711, CKAREN@SFTC.ORG

LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF NASER ODEH'S "MOTION FOR ENTRY OF JUDGMENT PURSUANT TO TERMS OF STIPULATED SETTLEMENT" IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. PLAINTIFF DEMANDS THAT DEFENDANT BE GIVEN ONLY "SEVEN DAYS" TO "CARRY OUT ALL THE TERMS OF THE SETTLEMENT." HOWEVER, ONE OF THE SETTLEMENT'S KEY TERMS IS THAT DEFENDANT PAY "ALL OUTSTANDING GOVERNMENTAL TAX LIENS." AT LEAST ONE TAX LIEN CANNOT BE PAID AT PRESENT BECAUSE STATE TAX AUTHORITIES HAVE YET TO PROVIDE THE REQUIRED CLEARANCE. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: RICHARD ULMER; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR# 8600 =302/HK

LAW AND MOTION 302, ATTORNEY CHARLES KATZ'S (UNOPPOSED) MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF NASER ODEH IS GRANTED. MR. KATZ TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED JUDICIAL COUNCIL ORDER. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; COURT REPORTER: MARIA A. TORREANO CSR#8600, EMAIL: MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Jan-24-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances.

RULING: There being no appearance, or objection, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on ATTORNEY CHARLES KATZ'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF NASER ODEH, as follows:

Charles Katz's unopposed motion to be relieved as counsel is granted.

Mr. Katz to prepare and submit a proposed judicial council order.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

Notice Of Motion To Enforce Settlement
Off Calendar Aug-03-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Atty. Charles Katz, Tel. No. appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. Troy D. Wigins of Wiggins Marks Rosenblatt, Tel. No. 1-415-840-7177 x 202
appearing for the Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D; Plaintiff Naser Odeh's "motion for entry of judgment pursuant to terms of stipulated settlement" is DENIED without prejudice. Plaintiff demands that defendant be given only "seven days" to "carry out all the terms of the settlement." However, one of the settlement's key terms is that defendant pay "all outstanding governmental tax liens." At least one tax lien cannot be paid at present because state tax authorities have yet to provide the required clearance. Order signed in open court. Judge: Richard Ulmer; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR# 8600 =302/hk

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Sep-06-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Charles J. Katz, Esq., Tel. 650.692.4100, for plaintiff;
Bradford A. Rosenblatt, Esq. of Wiggins Marks Rosenblatt, Tel. 415.840.7177 x210, for defendant Ismail Karajah.

RULING: The parties have not resolved the issues related to this motion. The matter was argued. At the end of the hearing, the Court issued its ruling on the OSC Re: Preliminary Injunction: Neither party may pay each other or themselves or any of their business affiliates with money from the restaurant. The restaurant shall be operated by Mr. Karajah. And if the case is dismissed, the court's order evaporates. [The Court's complete ruling is reflected in the court reporter's transcript.] Counsel for defendant shall submit a proposed order that complies with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Reyes F. Hunter, CSR#6576, rhunter@sftc.org.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Dissolution And Fix Value Of Shares Of Shareholders Seeking Dissolution
Off Calendar Jul-17-2017 Continued To Sep-27-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ISMAIL KARAJAH'S Motion To Stay Dissolution And Fix Value Of Shares Of Shareholders Seeking Dissolution is CONTINUED to September 27, 2017 at 9:30 AM in Department 302. The hearing on this motion is continued to give plaintiff Naser Odeh an opportunity to file a motion per Corporations Code 2000(f) to set a different valuation date and to give both parties the opportunity to provide evidence regarding the amount of the bond that Mr. Karajah needs to post. Mr. Odeh should notice his valuation date motion to be heard on September 27, 2017. That motion should also address the bond amount. Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/HK)
